item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So a lot of other SCP articles say the Foundation can do some crazy time travel crap. But no big deal, imo. Those articles happened in the future relative to us, and maybe this article was written like a decade in the past.

Either way, it makes me wonder just what the next objects will be. Especially the last black-white question mark. And what could the 6th object be that it's just floating in space between Pluto and Eris?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I freaking love this, man. Anomalous interplanetary geocaching? Sign me the heck up.

It's been said already, but I love that this is one of those things the foundation just can't get to quite yet because of its hilariously huge scope (in terms of distance at least). Don't worry, Astronaut Smith, we'll complete your treasure hunt eventually.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bet the prize is friendship. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, but the addenda and incident logs showing the year 2017 are really bothering me for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cosmic cubscout. how spooky. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just find this so damn charming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, but it's kind of anticlimatic. The Foundation can't really do anything about it right now, and there's no real sense of anything beyond what is presented. It's just an interstellar scavenger hunt that we can't really compete in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This punches all my buttons when it comes to space. +1, and Merry Christmas!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's kind of neat, and the implications are somewhat interesting, but overall, like some people have stated, it's just a little too scattered for an upvote. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the implication that there's another SCP on that dwarf planet (pattern of 1 pointing to 2, 2 pointing to 3, 3 pointing to some weird dwarf planet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Frickin' love this, and not just because anomalous wind sock. The idea that we know there are more parts to this out there and just can't get to them is great. (I have to wonder what the treasure is more than where it is, though. Spacetacular!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really bizarre. I can't quite wrap my head around a galactic scavenger hunt/carnival game. But it's really well written. The pacing is what makes it work, specifically. The description and containment procedure set up something really weird but it spends just the one addendum to make it work, and then wraps everything up very neatly with the message from O5-8. It's very trim, very breezy. It ramps up the weirdness, sticks the landing, and doesn't overstay its welcome.

I'm feeling a strong +1 on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I couldn't get into it too much, but it was well written. So, neutral vote.

One thing that I did notice was that you only talked about things that are basically in our solar system. We really can't see too far into our galaxy, because our view gets blocked. You could have really gone a little wild with your imagination and made up nearly anything you wanted to. No one could dispute you if they were actual locations in the Milky Way, or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Clues scattered around the whole galaxy?
That better be some treasure.
Interesting piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was interesting. I liked it. It wasn't a skip with a huge back story that may or may not have dire consequences or dark implications…it was just a for fun treasure hunt. Nice job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed "consistently" and "liaise", and added a necessary "to".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The purple text in the main list is a bit weird, but I like it. You should add a justification for it though - maybe a secondary anomalous effect of the SCP is that every time you write ASTRONAUT SMITH'S SPACE-TACULAR TREASURE HUNT!, it becomes purple and in all-caps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks Randomini for the idea, by the way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the concept but for some reason it just feels really scattered to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How on earth did they find the thing on Mars? Did Curiosity bump into it?

Still, it's cute and weird. Nice work.

Minor notes, I didn't change either of these in case my guesses were off:

reappear a random point

Reappear at a random point?

within a ██ years period

Within a ██ year period?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool beans. +1.
